questions.question: What is the 'Bridge Technique' when moving to a demo?

options.text: Explicitly linking the specific pain points uncovered in discovery to the specific features you will show in the demo

questions.explanation: The Bridge connects the 'Problem' phase to the 'Solution' phase. It ensures the prospect knows *why* they are watching the demo.

questions.question: If a prospect has Need and Budget, but NO Authority or Timeline, should you demo immediately?

options.text: No, this is a Nurture lead. You need to identify the Decision Maker before spending 60 minutes demoing

questions.explanation: Without Authority, you are pitching to a wallet that cannot open itself. You need to find the hand that opens the wallet.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Pre-Demo Recap Email'?

options.text: To confirm the specific priorities/pains so you can tailor the demo
To remind them of the time
To add them to your newsletter

questions.explanation: This email aligns expectations. If they agree to the pain points in writing, the demo becomes a solution to those agreed pains, not just a show.

questions.question: Why is 'Disqualifying' a positive action?

options.text: It allows you to be mean
It saves your time for qualified leads and builds credibility/status
It makes the CRM look clean

questions.explanation: Time is your scarcest resource. Investing time in bad leads is the fastest way to kill a startup.

questions.question: What is the minimum MEDDIC threshold to proceed to a demo?
questions.explanation: You can find the others later, but if there is no Pain (Need) and no Champion (Internal Interest), there is no motivation to buy.
